sections.title: Core Foundations
sections.description: Two tracks run in sequence: first get Go running, then learn the language primitives. The exercise synthesises all four language-basics lessons.

lessons.name: installation
lessons.concept: Verify Go binary; inspect GOROOT, GOPATH

lessons.name: hello-world
lessons.concept: package main · import · func main · fmt.Println

lessons.name: how-go-works
lessons.concept: Compilation model · packages · exported names

lessons.name: dev-environment
lessons.concept: go fmt · go vet · go build · go test · ./...

lessons.name: variables
lessons.concept: var · := · zero values · basic types

lessons.name: constants
lessons.concept: const · compile-time inlining · typed vs untyped

lessons.name: enums / iota
lessons.concept: iota · named types on int · const block

lessons.name: application logger
lessons.concept: Practical exercise combining variables, constants, and control flow

sections.title: Control Flow
sections.description: One loop construct, two branching constructs. The exercise requires all three in combination.

lessons.name: for loop
lessons.concept: C-style · while-style · infinite · range

lessons.name: if / else
lessons.concept: Guard clauses · if-with-init · comma-ok idiom

lessons.name: switch
lessons.concept: No fall-through · type switch · tagless switch

lessons.name: pricing calculator project
lessons.concept: Practical exercise combining loops, conditionals, and switch statements

sections.title: Data Structures
sections.description: Fixed arrays establish memory intuition. Slices build on that. Pointers unlock mutation. The advanced-slicing lesson requires both slices and pointers.

lessons.name: arrays
lessons.concept: Fixed-size contiguous memory · value-type copy

lessons.name: slices
lessons.concept: Slice header · make · append · capacity growth

lessons.name: maps
lessons.concept: Hash table · O(1) lookup · comma-ok · delete

lessons.name: pointers
lessons.concept: & · * · pass-by-value vs reference · nil

lessons.name: slices-2
lessons.concept: Sub-slicing · shared backing array · re-allocation trap

lessons.name: contact manager project
lessons.concept: Practical exercise combining arrays, slices, maps, and pointers

sections.title: Functions & Errors
sections.description: Functions first, then closures, then error-as-value, then cleanup. Defer must precede panic/recover. Error-wrapping builds on custom errors.

lessons.name: functions
lessons.concept: Parameters · return types · pass-by-value

lessons.name: closures & recursion
lessons.concept: Captured variables · anonymous functions · stack frames

lessons.name: variadic functions
lessons.concept: ...T syntax · slice internally · spread operator

lessons.name: multiple return values
lessons.concept: (value, error) convention · named returns · naked return

lessons.name: custom errors
lessons.concept: error interface · sentinel errors · errors.Is

lessons.name: error wrapping
lessons.concept: fmt.Errorf %w · errors.As · errors.Join

lessons.name: defer
lessons.concept: LIFO execution · argument evaluation timing · resource cleanup

lessons.name: panic & recover
lessons.concept: Stack unwinding · recover inside defer only

lessons.name: error handling project
lessons.concept: Practical exercise combining custom errors, wrapping, and defer cleanup

sections.title: Types & Interfaces
sections.description: Structs carry data; methods attach behaviour; interfaces name contracts. Stringer is the first interface you implement. Generics require understanding constraints, which build on interfaces.

lessons.name: structs
lessons.concept: Field layout · constructor pattern · value vs pointer copy

lessons.name: methods
lessons.concept: Value receiver vs pointer receiver · method sets

lessons.name: interfaces
lessons.concept: Implicit satisfaction · polymorphism · type assertions

lessons.name: Stringer
lessons.concept: fmt.Stringer · named type on int · custom Weekday

lessons.name: generics
lessons.concept: [T constraint] · comparable · union constraints · Map/Filter

lessons.name: payroll processor project
lessons.concept: Practical exercise combining structs, methods, interfaces, and generics

lessons.name: advanced generics
lessons.concept: Map/Filter patterns · constraint interfaces · reusable data pipelines

sections.title: Composition
sections.description: Named fields (composition) must precede anonymous fields (embedding) so the promotion behaviour has contrast. The exercise requires both.

lessons.name: composition
lessons.concept: Has-A vs Is-A · named field access · component reuse

lessons.name: embedding
lessons.concept: Anonymous fields · method promotion · field shadowing

sections.title: Strings & Text
sections.description: String internals first, then formatting verbs, then the byte/rune distinction, then regex, then templates. Each lesson assumes knowledge of the previous.

lessons.name: strings
lessons.concept: Immutable byte slice · strings package · strings.Builder

lessons.name: formatting
lessons.concept: fmt verbs · width/precision · Sprintf vs Printf vs Fprintf

lessons.name: unicode & runes
lessons.concept: UTF-8 multi-byte · rune · for-range vs byte iteration

lessons.name: regex
lessons.concept: MustCompile · FindAllString · capture groups · ReplaceAllStringFunc

lessons.name: text templates
lessons.concept: text/template · {{range}} · {{if}} · Execute to io.Writer

sections.title: Modules & Packages
sections.description: Module anatomy first, then add/remove/inspect commands, then semantic versioning and the replace directive. Strictly linear.

lessons.name: module basics
lessons.concept: go.mod anatomy · module path · go.sum checksums

lessons.name: managing deps
lessons.concept: go get · go mod tidy · go list · go mod why

lessons.name: versioning
lessons.concept: semver · v2 import path rule · replace · exclude · vendoring

lessons.name: build tags
lessons.concept: //go:build · conditional compilation · OS-specific code · test separation

sections.title: IO — Filesystem
sections.description: Basic file I/O before path manipulation before directories before temp files before embed. The io-patterns lesson depends on all three basic lessons (files, paths, directories). The testing-seam lesson requires embed and io-patterns.

lessons.name: files
lessons.concept: os.WriteFile · os.ReadFile · bufio.Scanner · os.OpenFile flags

lessons.name: paths
lessons.concept: filepath.Join · Base · Dir · Ext · Abs · Clean · Glob

lessons.name: directories
lessons.concept: MkdirAll · ReadDir · WalkDir · RemoveAll · os.Stat

lessons.name: temp files
lessons.concept: MkdirTemp · CreateTemp · unique names · immediate defer

lessons.name: embed
lessons.concept: //go:embed · string/[]byte · embed.FS · single-binary deployment

lessons.name: io.Reader / io.Writer patterns
lessons.concept: strings.Reader · bytes.Buffer · io.Copy · TeeReader · MultiWriter

lessons.name: fs.FS testing seam
lessons.concept: os.DirFS · fstest.MapFS · zero-disk-IO tests

sections.title: IO — Encoding
sections.description: Marshal before unmarshal. Streaming encoder/decoder build on the batch forms. Base64 is independent of streaming. The config-parser exercise requires all four core lessons.

lessons.name: JSON marshalling
lessons.concept: json.Marshal · MarshalIndent · struct tags · omitempty · "-"

lessons.name: JSON unmarshalling
lessons.concept: json.Unmarshal · pointer required · zero values · map[string]any

lessons.name: JSON encoder (stream)
lessons.concept: json.NewEncoder · io.Writer · O(1) memory · SetIndent

lessons.name: JSON decoder (stream)
lessons.concept: json.NewDecoder · io.Reader · NDJSON · EOF loop

lessons.name: Base64
lessons.concept: StdEncoding · URLEncoding · transport-safe binary

sections.title: IO — CLI Tools
sections.description: Raw args before typed flags before subcommand routing. The exercise combines all three plus filesystem skills.

lessons.name: args
lessons.concept: os.Args · os.Getenv · os.Exit codes · safe index access

lessons.name: flags
lessons.concept: flag.String/Int/Bool · flag.Parse() · pointer return · -help

lessons.name: subcommands
lessons.concept: flag.NewFlagSet · os.Args[2:] slicing · switch routing

sections.title: Web — Databases
sections.description: Connection before queries. Both INSERT and SELECT before prepared statements (which optimise them). Transactions require a working connection and basic queries. Repository pattern wraps everything.

lessons.name: connecting
lessons.concept: Blank import · sql.Open lazy · db.Ping · connection pool

lessons.name: query — INSERT
lessons.concept: db.Exec · ? parameterisation · LastInsertId · bcrypt hash

lessons.name: query — SELECT
lessons.concept: db.QueryRow · rows.Scan · rows.Close · rows.Err

lessons.name: prepared statements
lessons.concept: db.Prepare · stmt.ExecContext · when to prepare explicitly

lessons.name: transactions
lessons.concept: BeginTx · defer Rollback · Commit · ACID all-or-nothing

sections.title: Web — Masterclass
sections.description: Routing is the entry point. DI wraps routing. Templates need a working server. Middleware wraps the full mux. Auth requires sessions. CRUD requires auth + repository. Pagination and comments both require CRUD. Full-app synthesises all eleven lessons.

lessons.name: routing
lessons.concept: http.NewServeMux · method patterns · {param} · HandleFunc

lessons.name: dependency injection
lessons.concept: application struct · constructor · handler methods on struct

lessons.name: templates
lessons.concept: html/template · cache once · layout + partials + page

lessons.name: middleware
lessons.concept: func(http.Handler) http.Handler · chain · panic recovery

lessons.name: sessions
lessons.concept: Cookie-based sessions · flash messages · session store

lessons.name: authentication
lessons.concept: bcrypt · requireAuth middleware · context.WithValue user ID

lessons.name: forms
lessons.concept: Form struct · Required · MinLength · MatchesField · Valid()

lessons.name: posts CRUD
lessons.concept: PostRepository interface · ExecContext · pagination offset

lessons.name: pagination
lessons.concept: computeMetadata · LIMIT/OFFSET · HasNext/HasPrev · links

lessons.name: comments
lessons.concept: Adjacency list · 2-pass tree build · sync.RWMutex store

lessons.name: websockets
lessons.concept: HTTP Upgrade · gorilla/websocket · message pumping · real-time duplex

sections.title: Web — HTTP Client
sections.description: The naive client demonstrates the problem; the refactor lesson demonstrates the solution. Strictly sequential.

lessons.name: basic GET
lessons.concept: http.Get · resp.Body.Close · DefaultClient no-timeout problem

lessons.name: refactor for testability
lessons.concept: HTTPClient interface · IoTClient struct · NewIoTClient DI

sections.title: Concurrency — Goroutines & Channels
sections.description: Goroutines first. WaitGroups require goroutines. Channels require both. Buffered channels extend unbuffered. Closing extends buffered. Select requires all channel forms. Races and sync primitives are a parallel track that needs goroutines and basic channels. The pipeline project and downloader exercise require channels + closing.

lessons.name: goroutines
lessons.concept: go keyword · M:N scheduler · 2KB stack · closure-capture bug

lessons.name: WaitGroups
lessons.concept: Add · defer Done · Wait · pass by pointer rule

lessons.name: channels (unbuffered)
lessons.concept: make(chan T) · send · receive · block-until-ready · chan<- / <-chan

lessons.name: buffered channels
lessons.concept: make(chan T, N) · len vs cap · producer-consumer decoupling

lessons.name: closing channels
lessons.concept: close() · range over channel · comma-ok · broadcast signal

lessons.name: pipeline project
lessons.concept: ping/pong actors · select on done channel · context cancel

lessons.name: race conditions
lessons.concept: sync.Mutex · sync.RWMutex · sync/atomic · -race flag

lessons.name: select deep dive
lessons.concept: Multiplexing · timeout with time.After · non-blocking default · fan-in

lessons.name: sync primitives
lessons.concept: sync.Once singleton · sync.Map · when to use each

sections.title: Concurrency — Context
sections.description: Background is the root — all others derive from it. WithCancel adds manual cancellation. WithTimeout builds on that with an automatic deadline. WithValue adds metadata. The exercise requires all four forms.

lessons.name: Background & TODO
lessons.concept: Root context · Context interface (Deadline/Done/Err/Value)

lessons.name: WithCancel
lessons.concept: cancel func · ctx.Done() · goroutine leak prevention · tree propagation

lessons.name: WithTimeout
lessons.concept: Duration-based auto-cancel · WithDeadline · DeadlineExceeded

lessons.name: WithValue
lessons.concept: Private key type · request-scoped metadata · O(depth) lookup

sections.title: Concurrency — Time & Scheduling
sections.description: Time basics before formatting before timers and tickers. Random is independent of scheduling. Timezones require both basics and formatting. The scheduler requires tickers. The reminder exercise requires basics through timers.

lessons.name: time basics
lessons.concept: time.Time · Duration · Add · Sub · wall vs monotonic clock

lessons.name: formatting
lessons.concept: Reference Time "2006-01-02 15:04:05" · Parse · RFC3339

lessons.name: timers & tickers
lessons.concept: time.NewTimer · time.NewTicker · <-C · ticker.Stop() leak

lessons.name: random numbers
lessons.concept: rand/v2 · IntN · Shuffle · Perm · seeded PCG

lessons.name: scheduler
lessons.concept: Actor model · ScheduleOnce · ScheduleInterval · StopAll drain

lessons.name: timezones
lessons.concept: time.LoadLocation · IANA database · In() · always store UTC

sections.title: Concurrency Patterns
sections.description: errgroup basics before errgroup-with-context (which adds cancellation). sync.Pool is conceptually independent but must be taught alongside errgroup so the bounded exercise can combine both. Both exercises require all three foundation lessons.

lessons.name: errgroup basics
lessons.concept: errgroup.Group · g.Go · g.Wait · first-error semantics

lessons.name: errgroup + context
lessons.concept: WithContext · auto-cancel on first error · fan-out pipeline

lessons.name: sync.Pool
lessons.concept: Get → use → Reset → Put · GC eviction · zero-alloc buffers

lessons.name: bounded pipeline
lessons.concept: Semaphore + errgroup · backpressure · production-grade pipeline

lessons.name: url checker
lessons.concept: Practical exercise combining errgroup and context

lessons.name: robust worker pool
lessons.concept: Bounded concurrency · graceful shutdown · panic recovery · job queues

sections.title: Quality — Testing
sections.description: Unit tests establish the testing.T API. Table-driven extends that with sub-tests. HTTP handler testing requires both plus the httptest package. Benchmarking is independent but builds on table-driven instincts.

lessons.name: unit testing
lessons.concept: testing.T · t.Run · testify/assert · assert.NoError

lessons.name: table-driven tests
lessons.concept: []struct table · t.Run per row · data-isolated sub-tests

lessons.name: HTTP handler testing
lessons.concept: httptest.NewRecorder · httptest.NewRequest · no real server

lessons.name: benchmarking
lessons.concept: testing.B · b.ResetTimer · b.ReportAllocs · -benchmem

sections.title: Quality — HTTP Client Testing
sections.description: Manual mock establishes the pattern (interface → fake struct). Function-injection mock extends the fake with a GetFunc field. Table-driven mock applies §13 testing patterns to the function mock. Testify/mock replaces the hand-written mock with a library.

lessons.name: manual mock
lessons.concept: MockHTTPClient struct · io.NopCloser · injected interface

lessons.name: function-injection mock
lessons.concept: GetFunc field · GetCalls spy · per-test dynamic behaviour

lessons.name: table-driven mock
lessons.concept: []struct test matrix · sub-tests per case · errContains check

lessons.name: testify/mock
lessons.concept: mock.Mock embed · .On/.Return · AssertNumberOfCalls

sections.title: Quality — Profiling
sections.description: File-based CPU/memory profiling before live HTTP endpoint profiling. The HTTP endpoint lesson also requires familiarity with HTTP servers.

lessons.name: CPU profile
lessons.concept: pprof.StartCPUProfile · StopCPUProfile · go tool pprof · flat vs cum

lessons.name: live pprof endpoint
lessons.concept: net/http/pprof blank import · two-port pattern · goroutine leak

sections.title: Architecture — Package Design
sections.description: Naming conventions before export rules (which require understanding what exported means), before project layout (which presupposes both). Strictly linear.

lessons.name: naming
lessons.concept: Short lowercase names · no stutter · no utils · name by domain

lessons.name: visibility
lessons.concept: Uppercase = exported · internal/ compiler enforcement · minimal API

lessons.name: project layout
lessons.concept: cmd/ · internal/ · pkg/ · start flat · anti-patterns

sections.title: Architecture — Docker & Deployment
sections.description: Single-stage Dockerfile before multi-stage. Layer caching is fundamental to optimization. Compose orchestrates local development with multiple services.

lessons.name: single-stage Dockerfile
lessons.concept: FROM alpine · WORKDIR · COPY · RUN · ENTRYPOINT · minimal base image

lessons.name: multi-stage build
lessons.concept: Build stage · runtime stage · COPY --from · minimal final image

lessons.name: layer caching
lessons.concept: Dockerfile optimization · go.mod caching · order of commands · .dockerignore

sections.title: Architecture — Structured Logging
sections.description: slog basics before the context-keyed logger (which requires HTTP middleware knowledge). Custom handler requires both. zerolog comparison requires the custom handler for contrast. The exercise requires basics through custom handler.

lessons.name: slog basics
lessons.concept: TextHandler · JSONHandler · levels · typed attrs · With · Groups

lessons.name: context-keyed logger
lessons.concept: Private key type · FromContext · LoggingMiddleware · request_id

lessons.name: custom slog.Handler
lessons.concept: Enabled/Handle/WithAttrs/WithGroup · PrettyHandler · MultiHandler

lessons.name: zerolog comparison
lessons.concept: 0-alloc builder chain · when to choose zerolog vs slog

sections.title: Architecture — gRPC
sections.description: The proto definition generates code; the server and client lessons consume that generated code. Server must precede client so the service is running when the client lesson is taught.

lessons.name: proto definition + gen
lessons.concept: proto3 service/message · protoc · generated Go stubs

lessons.name: unary server
lessons.concept: Implement generated interface · status codes · interceptors · metadata

lessons.name: unary client
lessons.concept: grpc.NewClient · typed stub · context deadline · status.FromError

sections.title: Architecture — Graceful Shutdown
sections.description: OS signal handling is the entry point. HTTP drain builds on that by wiring shutdown into the server. The capstone combines both with a background worker and Kubernetes readiness probe pattern.

lessons.name: signal.NotifyContext
lessons.concept: SIGTERM · SIGINT · ctx cancel on signal · second Ctrl+C force-kill

lessons.name: HTTP graceful drain
lessons.concept: http.Server.Shutdown · ErrServerClosed · 30s deadline · readiness 503

sections.title: Architecture — Enterprise Capstone
sections.description: This is the curriculum's culminating project. Every previous section contributes at least one pattern. The Docker Compose boots Postgres, runs embedded migrations, and serves the REST API.

lessons.name: full REST API with Postgres
lessons.concept: Integration: routing, DI, auth, CRUD, Docker Compose, migrations, graceful shutdown

sections.title: Code Generation
sections.description: A single conceptual lesson. The //go:generate directive is the mechanism; mockery, stringer, and sqlc are the three canonical use cases demonstrated through it.

lessons.name: go:generate
lessons.concept: //go:generate directive · go generate ./... · mockery · stringer · sqlc
